title: RV8GR full T2 control opcode sweep
purpose: Prove the RV8GR T2 horizontal-control equations for every opcode and both initial Z states as a reusable circuit-level contract.

chips.role: IBUS/DBUS ownership and store/load bus safety
AC, Z, ALU result, and store buffer effect
PG and DP register load side effects
Concrete U33/U31 EI decode and IE state sink
Opcode-sweep observation, phase, clock, and bus-conflict monitors

executable_boundaries.contract: The opcode sweep drives IRH and T2 at the package boundary; control bits are the latched IRH bits documented by the ISA and wiring guide, not a physical or virtual chip.
executable_boundaries.mapping_rule: IRH[7:0] is the latched U5 control byte: [7]=ALU_SUB, [6]=XOR_MODE, [5]=MUX_SEL, [4]=AC_WR, [3]=SRC, [2]=STR, [1]=BR, [0]=JMP. Parent wires below bind each child port explicitly; no child port is connected by a matching-name rule.

wiring.description: U8 Q2 T2 drives every T2 consumer; VT observes only.
Opcode sweep bypasses fetch; this declared phase observation net is not a synthetic fetch proof.
Opcode sweep bypasses fetch; this declared phase observation net is not a synthetic fetch proof.
Latched U6 operand byte feeds U34, page/data controls, and the concrete PC load inputs.
U9 accumulator outputs feed the ALU feedback and U14 store buffer.
U21 Z output feeds the branch XOR path.
U23 page-register output boundary and concrete PC high-byte load input.
Explicit lower-seven-bit DP selection used by the address concatenation.
Explicit low-to-high address-wire concatenation: logical {DP,IRL} maps A0..A7=IRL and A8..A14=DP0..DP6.
Explicit U26-6 output export from BUS into the U33 page/data decode input.
U26-11 active-low PC-load strobe drives the concrete U1-U4 PC16 sink.
Shared documented reset input for PC and IE state.
PC16 state edge; T2 opcode vectors do not claim full fetch timing.
Virtual clock helper is isolated from functional control; it cannot supply a CPU state edge.
The current virtual BusProbe boundary is scalar; it remains isolated until it has an explicit eight-bit probe contract.
The current virtual BusProbe boundary is scalar; it remains isolated until it has an explicit eight-bit probe contract.
Observation-only assertion input; it has no authority to change CPU state.

behavior.scope: This circuit tests pure T2 execution logic and intentionally bypasses T0/T1 fetch.
behavior.ibus: SRC=1 selects RAM[{DP,IRL}], otherwise IRL immediate.
behavior.alu: XOR_MODE selects AC feedback or {8{ALU_SUB}}; MUX_SEL selects XOR output or adder sum.
behavior.ac: AC_WR=1 captures the selected ALU result; AC_WR=0 holds AC.
behavior.z: Z updates only when AC_WR=1; otherwise it holds the initial Z.
behavior.pg: PG loads IBUS when MUX_SEL=1 and AC_WR=0. Reserved mixes are visible and are not hidden by extra decode.
behavior.dp: DP loads IRL when XOR_MODE=1, SRC=0, STR=0, and AC_WR=0.
behavior.ie: IE sets when SRC=1, XOR_MODE=0, and AC_WR=0.
behavior.pc: PC loads {PG,IRL} when JMP=1 or BR=1 and Z matches ALU_SUB.
behavior.ram: STR=1 writes AC to RAM when the data address is in RAM page.

operation_harness.rule: The operation harness may drive only these real direction=input boundaries. It must not drive IBUS, DBUS, AC, Z, PG, DP, IE, or PC boundaries because they are shared buses or state outputs.

declared_clock_edges.source_detail: RV8GR wiring guide EI Decode: U33-8 EI_decode is wired to U31-3 1CLK. This flattened execution reference is valid only after explicit IEFF child materialization.
declared_clock_edges.scope: Functional simulator edge delivery only. It does not infer arbitrary output clocks or establish board-edge timing.

sample_points: control bits stable before T2 execution edge
bus ownership checked before write/read side effects
destination registers checked after model propagation
unsafe_states: reserved opcode mix changes PG, DP, IE, PC, RAM, or AC in a way not described by physical decode
SRC and STR overlap creates unreported bus contention
PC loads outside the documented branch/jump equation

guarantees: All 512 opcode/Z cases match the RV8GR Verilog opcode-sweep equations
Named reserved mixes are explicit and visible
Bus ownership remains conflict-free for the physical T2 equations
No test may reduce the full sweep to a PASS banner without assertions

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
